nodes.label: sanad-scanner-working.png
nodes.summary: Screenshot of the Sanad legal app Document Scanner view (ماسح المستندات). Shows a working camera viewfinder with activation controls, an upload-from-device alternative button, an empty scanned-pages section, a yellow best-practices tip banner, and the full RTL sidebar navigation. The app is installed as a PWA and targets Saudi lawyers and small legal practices.

nodes.label: Scanner View (ماسح المستندات)
nodes.summary: The Document Scanner view of Sanad allows users to photograph documents via device camera or upload an existing image, extract text via OCR, and export results as an image or Word file. An empty-state pages panel is shown when no pages have been captured yet.

nodes.label: OCR Text Extraction
nodes.summary: OCR (Optical Character Recognition) capability advertised in the scanner subtitle: extract text from photographed documents and export as image or Word file.

nodes.label: Camera Capture (تشغيل الكاميرا)
nodes.summary: Primary input method for the scanner: activates the device camera to photograph documents. Displayed as a large black viewfinder with a centered camera icon and an 'Activate Camera' button. Complemented by an upload-from-device alternative.

nodes.label: PWA Installation
nodes.summary: Sanad is installed as a Progressive Web App (PWA). The bottom-left badge reads 'Installed as a PWA app — pin it to your desktop for daily access.' The footer confirms the server is connected and the PWA is active.

nodes.label: Sanad RTL Sidebar Navigation
nodes.summary: Right-aligned RTL sidebar listing all top-level sections: Today, Dashboard, Clients, Cases Board, Documents, Document Scanner (active), Invoices, Compliance (badge 4), Tasks (badge 9), Deep Work, Calendar. Uses green highlight for active item.

nodes.label: ScannerView.tsx
nodes.summary: Inferred React component that renders the Document Scanner view shown in the screenshot. Likely handles camera activation, file upload, OCR trigger, and the pages list.
